skill.name: salesforce-automation
skill.description: Automate Salesforce tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, SOQL queries. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Salesforce CRM records, SOQL queries, and sales workflows through Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external-command and blocker findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, Salesforce tool names, and CRM object terminology. The confirmed risk is the required external Rube MCP endpoint and OAuth-backed Salesforce access, which can read and modify sensitive CRM records when authorized.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Endpoint Handles Salesforce Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add the Rube MCP endpoint and authenticate a Salesforce connection. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it routes CRM operations through an external service and OAuth-backed connector.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and Salesforce OAuth flow are explicitly documented. No malicious endpoint or covert exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Authorized Tools Can Modify Sensitive CRM Records
medium_findings.description: The documented workflows include creating and updating leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, tasks, arbitrary records, and record ownership. These are expected features, but misuse could alter business-critical Salesforce data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write-capable Salesforce tool names are directly listed. The risk is operational, not evidence of malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: SOQL Queries May Expose Broad CRM Data
medium_findings.description: The skill supports custom SOQL queries and pagination. Authorized users can query large Salesforce datasets, so deployments should rely on Salesforce permissions and review query scope.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Custom query capability and pagination are clearly documented. No hidden data transfer beyond the intended Salesforce connector was found.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The backtick detections occur in Markdown inline code and fenced SOQL examples that name Salesforce and Rube MCP tools. I found no executable Ruby, shell script, or command invocation in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is documentation, not code executed by the skill. The repository contains only SKILL.md for this skill.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance alerts are not supported by the reviewed lines. The cited content describes Salesforce automation, record identifiers, and Salesforce tool names.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain no crypto routines, host reconnaissance, or operating system commands. They are CRM workflow documentation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Networked Third-Party CRM Connector
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on an external MCP server for Salesforce automation. Users should only enable it in clients and workspaces where that connector is trusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoint is explicitly named. It is expected for the skill, but it is still a network trust boundary.

dangerous_patterns.title: High-Impact Salesforce Mutations
dangerous_patterns.description: The quick reference includes generic record creation and mass ownership transfer operations. These actions can have broad business impact if authorized users run them incorrectly.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented operations are powerful and state-changing. The finding is based on capability risk, not hidden malicious code.

content.value_statement: Salesforce work often requires repeated record searches, updates, and SOQL checks. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for using Rube MCP Salesforce tools.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Salesforce OAuth connection checks.
Lists Salesforce tools for leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, and tasks.
Documents required fields for common Salesforce record creation workflows.
Provides SOQL query patterns for contacts, relationships, and date filters.
Explains pagination handling for large Salesforce query results.
Summarizes quick-reference tool names and key parameters.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP and an active Salesforce connection before use.
Does not include executable scripts or direct API client code.
Depends on current Salesforce schema and user permissions.
Can affect production CRM data if users run write operations.

use_cases.title: Prepare Lead Updates
use_cases.description: Find leads, check required fields, and plan safe create or update steps.

use_cases.title: Query CRM Records
use_cases.description: Build SOQL queries for contacts, accounts, opportunities, and date-based filters.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Account Workflows
use_cases.description: Create accounts, associate contacts, and manage opportunity records through approved Salesforce tools.

prompt_templates.title: Check Salesforce Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Salesforce automation skill to verify Rube MCP is available and confirm whether the Salesforce connection is active.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Qualified Lead
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Salesforce automation skill to create a lead after confirming the required LastName and Company fields.

prompt_templates.title: Find Contacts by Account
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Salesforce automation skill to search for contacts linked to a specific account and summarize the matching records.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Opportunities with SOQL
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Salesforce automation skill to build a SOQL query for opportunities closing next month, then handle pagination if needed.

output_examples.input: Find recent leads for Acme and prepare an update.
output_examples.output: The assistant first searches available Salesforce lead tools, checks matching leads, confirms the target record, and asks before changing fields.

output_examples.input: Show contacts at technology accounts named Smith.
output_examples.output: The assistant prepares a SOQL contact query using account relationship fields and explains expected fields before running it.

output_examples.input: Create an opportunity for an existing account.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms account ID, opportunity name, stage, close date, and amount before calling the Salesforce creation tool.

best_practices: Search available Rube tools before each workflow to get current schemas.
Confirm Salesforce record IDs and required fields before write operations.
Use least-privilege Salesforce permissions and test risky workflows in a sandbox.
anti_patterns: Do not run broad SOQL queries without clear business scope.
Do not mass transfer ownership without explicit approval and a reviewed record list.
Do not assume display labels match Salesforce API field names.

faq.question: Does this skill connect directly to Salesforce?
faq.answer: It documents workflows that use Rube MCP and Composio Salesforce tools. The user must authorize the Salesforce connection.

faq.question: Can it create or update Salesforce records?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented tools include create and update workflows for leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, tasks, and generic records.

faq.question: Does it require API keys?
faq.answer: The skill states that users add the Rube MCP endpoint and complete Salesforce OAuth through the returned connection flow.

faq.question: Can it run SOQL queries?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes SOQL query guidance, relationship examples, date filters, and pagination notes.

faq.question: Is it safe for production Salesforce orgs?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill can guide write operations, so users should confirm records and use least-privilege permissions.

faq.question: What tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when Rube MCP is available.
